terms.definition: The parts of the face that make each person look different, such as eyes, nose, ears, mouth, and eyebrows.
terms.exampleUsage: Everyone has unique facial features that make them special.

terms.definition: Small dents or hollows on the cheeks that appear when some people smile.
terms.exampleUsage: Riya has cute dimples when she smiles.

terms.definition: The natural colour and appearance of a person's skin, especially on the face.
terms.exampleUsage: People from different parts of India have different complexions.

terms.definition: Being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else. Every person's face is unique.
terms.exampleUsage: Your fingerprints are unique - no one else in the world has the same ones.

terms.definition: To look like or be similar to someone else, especially a family member.
terms.exampleUsage: Many people say I resemble my mother because we have similar eyes.

terms.definition: Features like eye colour, hair type, or nose shape that children get from their parents.
terms.exampleUsage: Curly hair is an inherited trait that Anita got from her father.

terms.definition: The look on a person's face that shows what they are feeling, such as happy, sad, or surprised.
terms.exampleUsage: Her happy expression showed that she was excited about the trip.

terms.definition: Looking at something carefully to notice details and learn about it.
terms.exampleUsage: Through careful observation, the children noticed that everyone's ears are shaped differently.

terms.definition: Small brown spots on the skin, especially on the face, that some people have.
terms.exampleUsage: Some children have freckles on their nose and cheeks.

terms.definition: A small dark spot on the skin that some people are born with or develop over time.
terms.exampleUsage: Grandma has a small mole on her cheek that makes her easy to recognise.
